name: Scripting / System Pack

libraries.name: Cross-Platform Automation
libraries.description: Cross-Platform Automation scripts and system helpers.

groups.name: Script design checklist
groups.description: Script design checklist snippets and references.

functions.name: Reference — automation script design checklist
functions.insertText:
# 1. Validate required commands and input parameters.
# 2. Keep configuration outside the script when practical.
# 3. Fail fast on errors and propagate non-zero exit codes.
# 4. Quote paths and variables that may contain spaces.
# 5. Write logs with timestamps and enough context.
# 6. Make destructive operations explicit and reviewable.
# 7. Prefer idempotent operations for deployment scripts.
# 8. Add a dry-run mode when the script changes remote systems.
functions.description: Use this checklist before turning an ad-hoc command sequence into maintained automation.
functions.longDescription:
Use this checklist before turning an ad-hoc command sequence into maintained automation.

Source: Official tool documentation. Verify availability and version on the target operating system.

functions.name: Reference — portable project root convention
functions.signature: derive script root before using relative paths
functions.insertText:
# PowerShell
$ScriptRoot = $PSScriptRoot

# Bash
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

# CMD / Batch
set "SCRIPT_DIR=%~dp0"
functions.description: Derive paths from the script location rather than the caller current directory.
functions.longDescription:
Derive paths from the script location rather than the caller current directory.

Source: Official tool documentation. Verify availability and version on the target operating system.
functions.declaration: derive script root before using relative paths

functions.name: Recipe — fail fast and preserve exit status
functions.insertText:
# PowerShell
& cmake --build build
if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }

# Bash
cmake --build build || exit $?

# CMD
cmake --build build
if errorlevel 1 exit /b %errorlevel%
functions.description: Native tools signal errors with process exit codes. Preserve them in wrappers and CI jobs.
functions.longDescription:
Native tools signal errors with process exit codes. Preserve them in wrappers and CI jobs.

Source: Official tool documentation. Verify availability and version on the target operating system.

functions.name: Reference — idempotent deployment note
functions.insertText:
# Good deployment steps can be executed more than once:
# - mkdir -p / New-Item -Force
# - copy_if_different / rsync
# - declarative service configuration
# - explicit versioned migrations
# Avoid hidden mutable state and destructive wildcard deletes.
functions.description: Prefer repeatable operations so a partially failed deployment can be rerun safely.
functions.longDescription:
Prefer repeatable operations so a partially failed deployment can be rerun safely.

Source: Official tool documentation. Verify availability and version on the target operating system.

functions.name: Recipe — dry-run mode pattern
functions.insertText:
# Pseudocode shared by PowerShell and Bash automation:
# if dry_run:
#     print the command that would be executed
# else:
#     execute it and check the exit status
functions.description: Expose a dry-run option before scripts modify files, services, remote hosts, or repositories.
functions.longDescription:
Expose a dry-run option before scripts modify files, services, remote hosts, or repositories.

Source: Official tool documentation. Verify availability and version on the target operating system.

functions.name: console command advanced-picker guide
functions.insertText:
# Use the advanced picker to select documented command switches.
# Multi-select fields join compatible flags with spaces.
# Preserve snippets as recipes for multi-step workflows.
functions.description: Reference guide for structured command cards. Multi-selection is enabled only where switches are safely composable. Mutually exclusive policies remain separate single-choice fields.
functions.longDescription:
Reference guide for structured command cards. Multi-selection is enabled only where switches are safely composable. Mutually exclusive policies remain separate single-choice fields.

Source: JC Lib structured-pack design note

functions.name: Reference — console output redirection quick guide
functions.signature: Reference — console output redirection quick guide
functions.insertText:
# Windows CMD
# overwrite stdout: ipconfig /all > test.txt
# append stdout:    ipconfig /all >> test.txt
# stdout + stderr:  command > run.log 2>&1
# pipeline:         command1 | command2

# PowerShell
# overwrite output: Get-Process > processes.txt
# append output:    Get-Process >> processes.txt
# keep console + file: Get-Process | Tee-Object -FilePath processes.txt

# Bash / POSIX shell
# overwrite stdout: ip address show > network.txt
# append stdout:    ip address show >> network.txt
# stdout + stderr:  command > run.log 2>&1
# keep console + file: command | tee -a run.log
functions.description: Summarize the direction and semantics of common output-redirection operators across CMD, PowerShell and Bash.
functions.longDescription:
Summarize the direction and semantics of common output-redirection operators across CMD, PowerShell and Bash.

Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/cmd
Official reference documentation. Verify command availability, installed version, privileges and target-platform behavior before execution.
functions.declaration: Reference — console output redirection quick guide

functions.name: Recipe — environment variable configuration
functions.insertText:
# Never commit credentials in scripts.
# Typical names:
# APP_ENV
# APP_CONFIG_DIR
# DATABASE_URL
# API_TOKEN
# SSH_KEY_PATH
functions.description: Use environment variables or secret stores for deployment-time configuration. Do not print secrets in logs.
functions.longDescription:
Use environment variables or secret stores for deployment-time configuration. Do not print secrets in logs.

functions.name: Reference — dotenv caution note
functions.insertText:
# A .env file is convenient for local development only.
# Add it to .gitignore.
# Do not ship production credentials in repository files.
# Prefer the platform secret manager in CI and deployment systems.
functions.description: Keep .env files out of version control and distinguish local development from production secret management.
functions.longDescription:
Keep .env files out of version control and distinguish local development from production secret management.

Source: Official tool documentation. Verify availability and version on the target operating system.

functions.name: Reference — configuration precedence convention
functions.insertText:
# Recommended precedence from lowest to highest priority:
# 1. built-in defaults
# 2. configuration file
# 3. environment variables
# 4. explicit command-line arguments
functions.description: Document precedence so operators know which configuration source wins.
functions.longDescription:
Document precedence so operators know which configuration source wins.

functions.name: Recipe — retry with bounded exponential backoff
functions.insertText:
# Retry only transient failures.
# attempt_delay = min(initial_delay * 2**attempt, max_delay)
# Add jitter for large distributed systems.
# Stop after a bounded number of attempts or a deadline.
functions.description: Do not retry indefinitely. Distinguish transient connection errors from invalid configuration and authentication failures.
functions.longDescription:
Do not retry indefinitely. Distinguish transient connection errors from invalid configuration and authentication failures.

Source: Official tool documentation. Verify availability and version on the target operating system.

functions.name: Reference — lock file convention
functions.insertText:
# Windows PowerShell: open a FileStream with FileShare.None.
# Linux Bash: use flock on a dedicated file descriptor.
# Store a PID and timestamp when useful for diagnostics.
functions.description: Prevent concurrent deployment or maintenance scripts from corrupting shared resources.
functions.longDescription:
Prevent concurrent deployment or maintenance scripts from corrupting shared resources.

# PowerShell

functions.name: Recipe — archive commands cross-platform
functions.insertText:
# Linux archive
 tar -czf release.tar.gz -C release .
 tar -xzf release.tar.gz -C destination

# PowerShell ZIP
Compress-Archive -Path release\* -DestinationPath release.zip -Force
Expand-Archive -LiteralPath release.zip -DestinationPath destination -Force
functions.description: Create and extract release archives using platform-native tooling.

$job = Start-Job -ScriptBlock { Get-Process }
Wait-Job $job | Out-Null
$result = Receive-Job $job
Remove-Job $job

functions.name: Template — Batch robust skeleton
functions.insertText:
@echo off
setlocal EnableExtensions EnableDelayedExpansion

set "SCRIPT_DIR=%~dp0"
set "EXIT_CODE=0"

rem work here

exit /b %EXIT_CODE%
functions.description: Start a batch file with predictable echo, extensions, delayed expansion, and scoped environment changes.

exit /b 0

rem Variables: set "NAME=value", set /a, set /p
rem Conditions: if string1==string2, if EQU/NEQ/LSS/LEQ/GTR/GEQ, if exist, if defined, if errorlevel
rem Iteration: for, for /d, for /r, for /l, for /f
rem Arguments: %~1, %~f1, %~dp1, %%~fF
rem Scope and control: setlocal, endlocal, shift, call, goto, exit /b

functions.name: Recipe — Batch CMake release build
functions.insertText:
@echo off
setlocal
cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
if errorlevel 1 exit /b %errorlevel%
cmake --build build --parallel
if errorlevel 1 exit /b %errorlevel%
ctest --test-dir build --output-on-failure

  sleep 1
done
kill -KILL "$pid"

libraries.name: Linux systemd & Administration
libraries.description: Linux systemd & Administration scripts and system helpers.

groups.name: Service unit files
groups.description: Service unit files snippets and references.

functions.name: Template — systemd service unit robust
functions.insertText:
[Unit]
Description=Robot controller
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=robot
Group=robot
WorkingDirectory=/opt/robot-controller
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/robot-controller/controller.env
ExecStart=/opt/robot-controller/bin/controller
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2

NoNewPrivileges=true
PrivateTmp=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=true
ReadWritePaths=/var/lib/robot-controller /var/log/robot-controller

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
functions.description: Example systemd service with restart policy, external configuration, and baseline hardening.
functions.longDescription:
Example systemd service with restart policy, external configuration, and baseline hardening.

Source: systemd manual pages: https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/

functions.name: Recipe — systemd simple service minimal
functions.insertText:
[Unit]
Description=Device backend
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
WorkingDirectory=/opt/device-backend
ExecStart=/opt/device-backend/bin/server
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
functions.description: Minimal service unit for a long-running process.

functions.name: Template — systemd timer unit
functions.insertText:
# backup.timer
[Unit]
Description=Run device backup every hour

[Timer]
OnBootSec=5min
OnUnitActiveSec=1h
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

# backup.service
[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/device-backup.sh
functions.description: Schedule a service with a timer unit instead of embedding a permanent polling loop.

functions.name: Reference — SSH password login and secret handling
functions.signature: Reference — SSH password login and secret handling
functions.insertText:
ssh user@host
# Enter the account password only when OpenSSH prompts for it.
# Do not place a plaintext password in the command line or in a JC Lib parameter.
functions.description: Explain the safe OpenSSH password-login workflow: generate ssh user@host, then type the password only in the interactive prompt. Prefer key-based authentication for recurring automation.
functions.longDescription:
Explain the safe OpenSSH password-login workflow: generate ssh user@host, then type the password only in the interactive prompt. Prefer key-based authentication for recurring automation.

Source: https://man.openbsd.org/ssh.1

functions.longDescription:

Source: https://man.openbsd.org/ssh_config
Official reference documentation. Review platform requirements, privileges and generated values before execution.

parameters.name: alias

functions.declaration: Template — OpenSSH alias with identity file

functions.name: Recipe — append OpenSSH alias (Bash)
functions.insertText:
mkdir -p ~/.ssh
chmod 700 ~/.ssh
printf '
Host %s
  HostName %s
  User %s
  Port %s
  IdentityFile %s
  IdentitiesOnly yes
' "{{alias}}" "{{hostname}}" "{{user}}" "{{port}}" "{{identity}}" >> ~/.ssh/config
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/config
functions.description: Append one reusable OpenSSH alias to ~/.ssh/config from Bash. Inspect existing Host blocks first to avoid duplicates.
functions.longDescription:
Append one reusable OpenSSH alias to ~/.ssh/config from Bash. Inspect existing Host blocks first to avoid duplicates.

Source: https://man.openbsd.org/ssh_config
Official reference documentation. Review platform requirements, privileges and generated values before execution.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: Remote DNS name, hosts alias or IP.

functions.name: Recipe — append OpenSSH alias (PowerShell)
functions.insertText:
$sshDir = Join-Path $HOME ".ssh"
$config = Join-Path $sshDir "config"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $sshDir | Out-Null
@(
  ""
  "Host {{alias}}"
  "  HostName {{hostname}}"
  "  User {{user}}"
  "  Port {{port}}"
  "  IdentityFile {{identity}}"
  "  IdentitiesOnly yes"
) | Add-Content -Path $config
functions.description: Append one reusable OpenSSH alias to the current user configuration from PowerShell. Inspect existing Host blocks first to avoid duplicates.
functions.longDescription:
Append one reusable OpenSSH alias to the current user configuration from PowerShell. Inspect existing Host blocks first to avoid duplicates.

Source: https://man.openbsd.org/ssh_config
Official reference documentation. Review platform requirements, privileges and generated values before execution.

functions.name: Recipe — bootstrap SSH alias and Ed25519 key (Bash)
functions.signature: Recipe — bootstrap SSH alias and Ed25519 key (Bash)
functions.insertText:
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f "{{identity}}" -C "{{alias}}"
ssh-copy-id -i "{{identity}}.pub" "{{user}}@{{hostname}}"
printf '
Host %s
  HostName %s
  User %s
  IdentityFile %s
  IdentitiesOnly yes
' "{{alias}}" "{{hostname}}" "{{user}}" "{{identity}}" >> ~/.ssh/config
ssh -G "{{alias}}"
functions.description: Generate an Ed25519 client key, deploy only its public half to a remote Linux account, append a local SSH alias and inspect the effective configuration.
functions.longDescription:
Generate an Ed25519 client key, deploy only its public half to a remote Linux account, append a local SSH alias and inspect the effective configuration.

Source: https://man.openbsd.org/ssh_config
Official reference documentation. Review platform requirements, privileges and generated values before execution.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: Remote hostname, hosts alias or IP.

parameters.name: identity
parameters.description: New local private-key path. The .pub file is deployed remotely.

functions.declaration: Recipe — bootstrap SSH alias and Ed25519 key (Bash)

functions.name: Recipe — bootstrap SSH alias and Ed25519 key (PowerShell)
functions.signature: Recipe — bootstrap SSH alias and Ed25519 key (PowerShell)
functions.insertText:
$sshDir = Join-Path $HOME ".ssh"
$config = Join-Path $sshDir "config"
$identity = "{{identity}}"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $sshDir | Out-Null
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f $identity -C "{{alias}}"
Get-Content "$identity.pub" | ssh "{{user}}@{{hostname}}" "umask 077; mkdir -p ~/.ssh; cat >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys"
@(
  ""
  "Host {{alias}}"
  "  HostName {{hostname}}"
  "  User {{user}}"
  "  IdentityFile {{identity}}"
  "  IdentitiesOnly yes"
) | Add-Content -Path $config
ssh -G "{{alias}}"
functions.description: Generate an Ed25519 client key on Windows or PowerShell, deploy only the public key to a Linux-style remote account, append a local SSH alias and inspect it. The private key stays local.
functions.longDescription:
Generate an Ed25519 client key on Windows or PowerShell, deploy only the public key to a Linux-style remote account, append a local SSH alias and inspect it. The private key stays local.

Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/openssh/openssh_keymanagement
Official reference documentation. Review platform requirements, privileges and generated values before execution.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: Remote hostname, hosts alias or IP.

parameters.name: identity
parameters.description: New local private-key path. Keep this file private.

functions.declaration: Recipe — bootstrap SSH alias and Ed25519 key (PowerShell)

functions.name: Reference — local hosts alias versus SSH Host alias
functions.signature: Reference — local hosts alias versus SSH Host alias
functions.insertText:
Local resolver alias:
  raspberrypi -> 192.168.1.42
  Windows: %SystemRoot%\System32\drivers\etc\hosts
  Linux: /etc/hosts

OpenSSH client alias:
  Host myraspi
    HostName raspberrypi
    User pi
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_myraspi

Connection:
  ssh myraspi
functions.description: Explain the difference between local name resolution and the richer per-user OpenSSH alias configuration. A hosts entry maps a name to an IP. A Host block can additionally bind the remote account, port and private key.
functions.longDescription:
Explain the difference between local name resolution and the richer per-user OpenSSH alias configuration. A hosts entry maps a name to an IP. A Host block can additionally bind the remote account, port and private key.

Source: https://man.openbsd.org/ssh_config
Official reference documentation. Review platform requirements, privileges and generated values before execution.
functions.declaration: Reference — local hosts alias versus SSH Host alias

functions.name: Recipe — Bash remote deploy archive
functions.insertText:
scp release.tar.gz robot@192.168.1.50:/tmp/
ssh robot@192.168.1.50 '
  set -e
  sudo mkdir -p /opt/robot-controller
  sudo tar -xzf /tmp/release.tar.gz -C /opt/robot-controller
  sudo systemctl restart robot-controller
'
functions.description: Upload one archive, extract remotely, and restart a service.

cat > commands.txt <<'EOF'
put release.tar.gz /tmp/release.tar.gz
ls -l /tmp/release.tar.gz
EOF

sftp -b commands.txt robot@192.168.1.50

functions.name: Recipe — inspect logs and enter a container
functions.insertText:
docker ps --all
docker inspect device-api
docker logs --follow device-api
docker exec -it device-api sh
functions.description: Inspect and debug running containers.
functions.longDescription:
Inspect and debug running containers.

Source: Docker documentation: https://docs.docker.com/

functions.name: Recipe — compress and restore an image archive
functions.insertText:
docker save device-api:1.0.0 | gzip > device-api_1.0.0.tar.gz
gunzip -c device-api_1.0.0.tar.gz | docker load
functions.description: Transfer an image to an offline or constrained target.
functions.longDescription:
Transfer an image to an offline or constrained target.

Source: Docker documentation: https://docs.docker.com/

functions.name: Reference — review Docker prune impact
functions.insertText:
docker system df
# Review before use:
docker system prune
functions.description: Inspect disk usage before pruning unused resources.
functions.longDescription:
Inspect disk usage before pruning unused resources.

Source: Docker documentation: https://docs.docker.com/

functions.name: Recipe — Bash Docker build test
functions.insertText:
docker build -t device-app:test .
docker run --rm device-app:test ./run-tests.sh
functions.description: Build and run containerized tests.
functions.longDescription:
Build and run containerized tests.

Source: GNU Bash Reference Manual: https://www.gnu.org/software/bash/manual/bash.html

functions.name: Template — Dockerfile multi-stage build
functions.insertText:
FROM node:22-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1/ || exit 1
functions.description: Use multi-stage builds to keep runtime images smaller and separate build dependencies.
functions.longDescription:
Use multi-stage builds to keep runtime images smaller and separate build dependencies.

Source: Docker documentation: https://docs.docker.com/

functions.name: Template — Dockerfile non-root service
functions.insertText:
FROM debian:stable-slim
RUN useradd --system --create-home app
WORKDIR /opt/app
COPY --chown=app:app ./bin/server ./server
USER app
ENTRYPOINT ["./server"]
functions.description: Run the application as a dedicated non-root user when practical.
functions.longDescription:
Run the application as a dedicated non-root user when practical.

Source: Docker documentation: https://docs.docker.com/

functions.name: Template — .dockerignore baseline
functions.description: Keep secrets, local dependencies, and build artifacts out of the Docker build context.
functions.longDescription:

functions.name: Template — Compose application stack
functions.insertText:
services:
  api:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://app:${DB_PASSWORD}@db/app
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped

  db:
    image: postgres:17
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: app
      POSTGRES_USER: app
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U app -d app"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  postgres-data:
functions.description: Define a multi-container stack with a persistent volume and health-dependent startup ordering.
functions.longDescription:
Define a multi-container stack with a persistent volume and health-dependent startup ordering.

Source: Docker documentation: https://docs.docker.com/

functions.name: Reference — Compose named-volume backup note
functions.insertText:
# Back up named volumes according to the application consistency requirements.
# For databases, prefer engine-native backup commands where available.
functions.description: Do not treat raw volume copies as universally safe database backups.
functions.longDescription:
Do not treat raw volume copies as universally safe database backups.

functions.name: Recipe — GitHub Actions basic CI
functions.insertText:
name: ci

on:
  push:
  pull_request:

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
      - run: cmake --build build --parallel
      - run: ctest --test-dir build --output-on-failure
functions.description: Basic CI workflow for a CMake project. Pin action versions according to repository policy.
functions.longDescription:
Basic CI workflow for a CMake project. Pin action versions according to repository policy.

# Inject them only into the jobs that require them.

functions.insertText:

if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }
cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build --parallel
Compress-Archive -Path build\Release\* -DestinationPath release.zip -Force
